text:
The officialOlympicsaccount appears to have removed the Paris 2024 opening ceremony footage from its YouTube page after the event received widespread criticism.

A number of social media users posted screenshots from their devices showing the message 'this video is not available' when trying to watch the coverage back.

The Olympics and Paris 2024 have not issued a statement to explain why the video is not available.

The opening ceremony raised eyebrows with its unconventional style. It involved models, dancers, fashion icons and drag queens as well as a range of artistic themes.

A scene recreating Leonardo Da Vinci's 'The Last Supper'with drag queens and other performers was among the moments that sparked the biggest reaction.

READ MORE:Olympics row escalates as sponsor pulls out over 'blasphemous' opening ceremony



Tesla and X bossElon Muskwas among the high-profile voices to condemn the ceremony.

He said: "Christianity has become toothless."

Another moment in the show that caught the eye was a giant platter of fake fruits and other foods with a smurf-looking person sitting on top of them.

The man was dressed as the Greek God of wine, festivity and theatre, Dionysus.

Don't miss:Olympics ceremony blasted 'extremely disrespectful' with millions insulted

Don't miss:Olympics viewers call for 'genius' Danny Boyle to take over opening ceremony



He dismissed criticism of the show the day after, saying: "It wouldn't be fun if there were no controversy. Wouldn't it be boring if everyone agreed on this planet?"

The New York Times branded the ceremony a "bloated made-for-TV spectacle" while the New York Post said it was "boring, ill-conceived and choppy."

Meanwhile, French newspaper Le Figaro added that the show was "great but some of it was just too much."

title: Paris Olympics opening ceremony deleted from YouTube after outrage | World | News | Express.co.uk
author: Charlie Bradley
description: The opening ceremony for Paris 2024 has come under fire due to controversial scenes and other bizarre moments.
